name: Pocket Pork Chops
recipeIngredient: 1/2 each Small apple, peeled and chopped
2 each 1 inch thick pork loin chops
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F.
2. In a small skillet, heat chicken stock and sauté onion, mushrooms, and apples until translucent.
3. In a small bowl or cup, pour boiling water over raisins. Set aside for a few minutes until plumped. Drain.
4. Stir in the plumped raisins, bread crumbs, parsley, and seasonings into the mixture in the skillet.
5. Cut a deep pocket in each pork chop. Fill with stuffing and secure with wooden picks.
6. In a skillet coated with non-stick spray, brown chops quickly on both sides.
7. Place chops side by side in a small baking dish. Pour just enough water to cover the bottom of the dish.
8. Cover and bake for 35 to 45 minutes, or until done.
9. Serve chops with applesauce.
To complete menu: Steam and mash 1 1/2 small potatoes and 1/2 cup sliced green beans.
NOTE: To make your own applesauce, just peel, core, and slice an apple and cook in 2 tablespoons of water with a dash each of cinnamon and cloves. Mash the apple when tender, and voila! Instant apple sauce.
